gripper_channel_analysis.name: state.main_gripper
gripper_channel_analysis.interpretation: Gripper distribution not clearly bimodal under this heuristic; review before filtering.

gripper_channel_analysis.name: action.main_gripper
gripper_channel_analysis.interpretation: Gripper appears concentrated near open/closed (or multi-modal). Statistical outlier rates on this channel often reflect normal grasp transitions rather than sensor corruption — do not delete normal grasp transitions.

state_boundary_saturation.name: main_shoulder_pan
state_boundary_saturation.caveat: bounds are empirical dataset min/max, not known hardware joint limits

teleoperation.missing_value_note: parquet float columns: NaN counted explicitly; no separate null mask beyond NaN for these arrays

low_entropy_threshold.note: low entropy ≠ proven occlusion; may be blank, blur, or close-up self-occlusion

occlusion_interpretation_guide.unusable_camera_occlusion: near-zero entropy + near-black/white for sustained runs
occlusion_interpretation_guide.expected_self_occlusion: gripper/object fills frame during grasp; still informative
occlusion_interpretation_guide.low_info_background: empty table / motion away from workspace

egocentric.duplicate_categories_note: Mutually exclusive categories with corrected definitions

duplicate_while_state_changes.note: Duplicate visuals while state changes suggest frozen/stream stutter or encode reuse — soft review flag, not automatic corruption.

low_info_near_grasp_proxy.note: Elevated low-information rates near large gripper deltas may reflect expected close-up grasp / self-occlusion, not unusable camera failure.

interpretation_rules: Do not treat expected gripper self-occlusion during grasp as corruption.
Isolated single-frame blur spikes coincident with fast motion are soft review flags.
Sustained blur/low-info intervals are stronger soft flags for shared-timestep masking.
Never delete a wrist frame independently of its state/action timestep.

duplicate_recount_within_episode.note: Mutually exclusive within-episode adjacent pairs only (group by episode, sort by frame_index, consecutive only). Never across episode boundaries. Counts taken from per-episode curation flags (identical thresholds); legacy Task-1 stream used denom=19630 and near=7424 by including 49 cross-episode pairs (19 of which were near_duplicate).
